refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour or mass per hour.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on supporting achievement of a productivity target. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how to achieve it, only what is to be achieved. The units are given as examples (m³/h or ton/h) to clarify the metric without implying implementation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through operational data collection during representative operations that the NGHE solution enables achieving the customer-defined productivity target as measured in volume or mass per hour over the agreed evaluation period.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume per hour.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined productivity target measured in mass per hour.

candidate_dps: Provide a controllable throughput mechanism to regulate processing speed
Implement a feedback system to monitor and adjust productivity in real-time
Design modular process stages that can be independently optimized to meet productivity targets
Incorporate scalable resource allocation to match customer-defined productivity levels
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a feedback system to monitor and adjust productivity in real-time

potential_sources: Tight coupling between feedback system and throughput mechanism if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a feedback system DP supports independence by allowing modular adjustments without redesigning the entire throughput mechanism. Care must be taken to keep monitoring and control logic decoupled from physical processing components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable productivity measurement methods during operations
Risk that feedback system complexity may introduce latency affecting responsiveness
Assumes operational policies allow dynamic adjustment of processing parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining measurement and control sub-functions to clarify interfaces and reduce coupling risk between monitoring and throughput control.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for implementing productivity monitoring and adjustment features, including feedback control logic and data processing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from sensors and sends processed productivity metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends adjustment commands to operational units and receives task status updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication between distributed components for feedback coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the feedback system is central to the solution; minimal diffusion since it integrates data collection and control.
Partial containment possible as vehicle sensors and actuators are involved, but feedback logic would diffuse outside.
Limited containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not productivity feedback.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety, not productivity feedback.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the feedback system DP because it centralizes the monitoring and adjustment logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may lead to coupling with Vehicle module for sensor data.
Real-time data dependencies may require careful interface management to avoid latency issues.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing a feedback system to monitor and adjust productivity in real-time directly addresses the FR of supporting achievement of the customer-defined productivity target measured in volume or mass per hour. The DP logically fulfills the functional requirement by enabling real-time control to meet productivity goals.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for sensor data may cause diffusion if sensor data handling is not well encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating that the feedback system DP is well contained within a single implementation module. Interfaces to Vehicle and other modules are managed via defined external interfaces, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring reported productivity metrics during representative operations to ensure productivity meets or exceeds the target over the evaluation period, as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate NGHE Solution module clearly encapsulates the DP implementation, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry, tasking, and network communication, providing clear allocation of responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface specifications with the Vehicle module to ensure sensor data integration does not cause diffusion or latency issues.
Document latency and real-time data handling requirements explicitly in interface contracts to mitigate risks associated with real-time feedback control.
Consider modularizing sensor data acquisition within NGHE Solution or as a dedicated submodule to reduce coupling with Vehicle module.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating that the current FR, DP, and IM mapping is logically sound and well contained, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks with the Vehicle module sensor data integration necessitate revisions. Clarifying interface specifications, documenting latency and real-time data handling requirements, and considering modularization of sensor data acquisition will mitigate diffusion and latency risks, which is critical for safety and system reliability. Therefore, revisions at the current level are required before proceeding to derive next-level sub-FRs.

revise.why: To clarify interface specifications with the Vehicle module, explicitly document latency and real-time data handling requirements, and consider modularizing sensor data acquisition to reduce coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation module for the feedback system DP.
Interfaces to Vehicle and other modules are currently defined but require refinement to address latency and diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for sensor data may cause diffusion if not well encapsulated.
Latency and real-time data handling requirements may not be fully captured, risking feedback control performance.
evidence_needed: Updated interface specifications with Vehicle module showing latency and data handling requirements.
Design documentation on modularization approach for sensor data acquisition within NGHE Solution or as a dedicated submodule.
